hallucination.rationale: The note states 'Gastrointestinal: Reports no gastrointestinal symptoms' which was never discussed in the transcript. 'Patient appears fatigued' under General is an embellishment — the transcript does not describe the patient's appearance. The Medications section lists post-plan medications (dapagliflozin, atorvastatin 80mg) as current medications in the Subjective section, which is misleading. HCTZ is missing from the medication list.

instruction_following.rationale: The template instructs to only include ROS systems that were discussed and to omit undiscussed ones, yet Gastrointestinal was included despite not being discussed. The template says to only include vitals that were documented, yet HR, Temp, RR, SpO2 are listed as 'Not documented' instead of being omitted. The note also includes template bracket/parenthetical instructions implicitly violated.

completeness.rationale: Several important details are missing: weight (234 lbs), BMI (33.5), height (5'10"), creatinine (1.8), GFR (42), urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (220), LDL (98), triglycerides (210), monofilament testing results, no ankle edema, pedal pulses intact, no open sores/calluses, heart sounds regular/no murmurs, lungs clear, diabetic peripheral neuropathy diagnosis, back pain history, counseling on positional changes with HCTZ, counseling on interconnected conditions, wife's involvement, dialysis discussion, post-meal sugar checking instruction, low sodium diet counseling, Dr. Khatri coordination, target BP of 130/80, after-visit summary printed.
missed_items: Counseling on positional changes with HCTZ

template_adherence.rationale: The overall structure follows the template well with appropriate sections. Minor issues include listing undocumented vitals instead of omitting them, and the neurological symptoms (numbness/neuropathy) being placed under Musculoskeletal in ROS rather than Neurological.

entity_marking.rationale: Entity marking syntax is correct, but drugs are re-marked on subsequent occurrences throughout the note (e.g., amlodipine, losartan, metformin, tresiba, dapagliflozin, atorvastatin all marked multiple times). Several conditions are not marked (diabetic peripheral neuropathy, dyslipidemia/hyperlipidemia). The duplication of the entire note compounds the re-marking issue.

duplication.rationale: The entire clinical note is duplicated — the full note appears twice in the output.
